name: Paraguay
excerpt: Several Indigenous groups, principally belonging to the Guarani language family, inhabited the area of modern Paraguay before the arrival of the Spanish in the early 16th century, when the territory was incorporated into the Viceroyalty of Peru.

quick_facts.religions: Roman Catholic 80.4%, Protestant 7% (Evangelical (non-specific) 6.7%, Evangelical Pentecostal <0.1%, Adventist <0.1%, Protestant (non-specific) <0.1%), Believer (not belonging to the church) 5.7%, other 0.6%, agnostic <0.1%, none 0.2%, unspecified 6.2% (2023 est.)
quick_facts.ethnic_groups: Mestizo (mixed Spanish and Indigenous ancestry) 95%, other 5%
quick_facts.climate: subtropical to temperate; substantial rainfall in the eastern portions, becoming semiarid in the far west
quick_facts.terrain: grassy plains and wooded hills east of Rio Paraguay; Gran Chaco region west of Rio Paraguay mostly low, marshy plain near the river, and dry forest and thorny scrub elsewhere
quick_facts.natural_resources: hydropower, timber, iron ore, manganese, limestone
quick_facts.major_exports: soybeans, beef, electricity, corn, soybean meal (2023)

summaries.geography_summary: landlocked; lies between Argentina, Bolivia, and Brazil; population concentrated in eastern and southern part of country
summaries.history_summary: Several Indigenous groups, principally belonging to the Guarani language family, inhabited the area of modern Paraguay before the arrival of the Spanish in the early 16th century, when the territory was incorporated into the Viceroyalty of Peru. Paraguay achieved its independence from Spain in 1811 with the help of neighboring states. In the aftermath of independence, a series of military dictators ruled the country until 1870. During the disastrous War of the Triple Alliance (1864-70) -- fought against Argentina, Brazil, and Uruguay -- Paraguay lost two thirds of its adult males and much of its territory. The country stagnated economically for the next half-century and experienced a tumultuous series of political regimes. Following the Chaco War of 1932-35 with Bolivia, Paraguay gained a large part of the Chaco lowland region. The 35-year military dictatorship of Alfredo STROESSNER ended in 1989, and Paraguay has held relatively free and regular presidential elections since the country's return to democracy.
summaries.economy_summary: upper middle-income South American economy; COVID-19 hit while still recovering from 2019 Argentina-driven recession; global hydroelectricity leader; major corruption and money-laundering locale; highly agrarian economy; significant income inequality

suggested_reading: CIA World Factbook data (archived via factbook.json) | https://github.com/factbook/factbook.json

prayer_and_mission.prayer_intro: Paraguay has one of the highest Catholic percentages in South America, with evangelical and Protestant believers remaining a small minority. Pray for the clear gospel of salvation by grace through faith alone to break through generations of inherited Catholic tradition.
prayer_points: Pray for laborers to plant independent, Bible-believing local churches across a country where evangelical presence remains very small.
Pray for Paraguayan pastors to be trained in expository, Scripture-centered preaching.
Pray for Paraguayans who identify as believers without formal church involvement to be connected to sound biblical teaching and genuine local church membership.
Pray for rural and Guaraní-speaking communities, often shaped strongly by folk-Catholic tradition, to hear the gospel in a way they can understand and receive.
prayer_and_mission.mission_emphasis: With over 80 percent of Paraguayans identifying as Roman Catholic and evangelical believers a small minority, Paraguay represents a significant gospel need in South America. Ongoing needs include training national pastors in sound biblical doctrine, planting autonomous local churches practicing believer's baptism by immersion, and reaching rural and Guaraní-speaking communities where folk-Catholic tradition remains deeply embedded.
